candidates.why: Separate operating depreciation lines appear below Cost of Goods Sold and other operating expense components, not as a labelled sub-component of Cost of Goods Sold. However, nothing in the excerpt ties them specifically to COGS, so assigning them to COGS would be speculative and risks double counting or misclassification.
This appears to be a header/summary label, but it has no visible values and does not clearly indicate a separate COGS-related D&A amount to add.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Cost of Goods Sold'. The excerpt shows separate D&A lines at the operating-expense level, but no row explicitly links D&A to COGS or cost of sales. Conservatively do not add any COGS_DA row.

candidates.why: Depreciation is shown as a separate operating line item at the same level as the selected SG&A-like components rather than as a sub-component nested within them, so it appears outside the selected XSGA bucket and would need to be added separately if used in PROF construction.
A possible summary label exists, which would normally be preferred over components, but it has no visible values in the excerpt, so it does not function as a usable summary row here.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket consists of overhead component rows and excludes depreciation/amortisation. A separate 'Depreciation' line appears at the same operating level, suggesting it should be added separately to XSGA. Manual review is needed because the label 'Depreciation' appears more than once in the excerpt and the statement does not clearly distinguish whether all or only part of depreciation belongs to SG&A versus other operating buckets; no usable non-impairment amortisation-only row is clearly available.
